book_summary: Exodus has begun with chapters 1-4 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The opening block preserves names against erasure, Israel's creation-blessing multiplication, Pharaoh's fear-based oppression, Shiphrah and Puah as named life-protectors, Moses' water-rescue through women's agency, Divine hearing/remembering/seeing/knowing, burning bush, YHWH name disclosure as I AM / I WILL BE, liberation commission, speech accommodation through Aaron, and Zipporah's difficult circumcision episode with ambiguity preserved.

chapters.notes: Names, multiplication, forced labor, midwives' resistance, Pharaoh's death-command.
Moses birth/rescue, women's agency, Moses' complicated intervention, Midian, Divine hearing covenant groaning.
Burning bush, holy ground, YHWH name disclosure, Divine seeing/hearing/knowing, liberation commission.
Signs, Moses' speech objection, Aaron as support, return to Egypt, firstborn confrontation, Zipporah episode, elders believe.

translation_notes: Hebrew-first approach required: Shemot/Names, YHWH, Elohim, avad/avodah, berit, ruach, pesach, mishkan, kavod, chesed where relevant.
Avad/avodah is a central thread: Pharaoh turns service into crushing bondage; YHWH liberates for covenant service/worship.
YHWH name disclosure in Exodus 3:14 preserves Hebrew verbal ambiguity: I AM / I WILL BE.
Pharaoh hardening language must track Hebrew verbs carefully: hazaq, kaved, qashah require later lexical audit.
Do not demonize Egyptians ethnically; critique Pharaoh, empire, forced labor, and fear-based domination.
Women's agency is central in Exodus 1-2 and should remain visible.
Zipporah's circumcision episode is obscure; preserve ambiguity and avoid making it a ritual template.
Speech/disability material must not shame Moses; the narrative gives presence, teaching, and Aaron as accommodation/partnership.
Land promise and named peoples require later ethical audit, especially before conquest material.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Exodus 3:14 YHWH/Ehyeh asher ehyeh requires deep lexical and reception-history audit.
Exodus 4:21 and hardening motif should receive verb-by-verb audit across Exodus.
Exodus 4:24-26 Zipporah circumcision episode requires source, syntax, and reception-history note.
Exodus land promise / named peoples requires ethical and historical reception audit before later conquest texts.
next_actions: Continue Exodus with chapters 5-12 as next major block: Pharaoh confrontation, increased oppression, YHWH covenant reassurance, signs/plagues, Passover, and liberation night.
